decoder_heading: Do we use 'cookies'?
ident_heading: Do we use 'cookies'?
heading_content_part: Do we use 'cookies'?Yes. Cookies are small files that a site or its service provider transfers to your computer's hard drivethrough your Web browser (if you allow) that enables the site's or service provider's systems torecognize your browser and capture and remember certain information. For instance,we use cookies to help us remember and process the items in your shopping cart.They are also used to help us understand your preferences based on previous or current site activity,

decoder_heading: Third-party disclosure
ident_heading: Third-party disclosure
heading_content_part: Third-party disclosureWe do not sell, trade, or otherwise transfer to outside parties your Personally Identifiable Informationunless we provide users with advance notice. This does not include website hosting partners andother parties who assist us in operating our website, conducting our business, or serving our users,so long as those parties agree to keep this information confidential. We may also release information

decoder_heading: Third-party disclosure

heading_content_part: when it's release is appropriate to comply with the law, enforce our site policies, or protect ours orothers' rights, property or safety.However, non-personally identifiable visitor information may be provided to other parties for marketing,advertising, or other uses.

decoder_heading: Third-party links
ident_heading: Third-party links
heading_content_part: Third-party linksOccasionally, at our discretion, we may include or offer third-party products or services on our website.These third-party sites have separate and independent privacy policies.We therefore have no responsibility or liability for the content and activities of these linked sites.Nonetheless, we seek to protect the integrity of our site and welcome any feedback about these sites.

decoder_heading: COPPA (Children Online Privacy Protection Act)
ident_heading: COPPA (Children Online Privacy Protection Act)
heading_content_part: COPPA (Children Online Privacy Protection Act)When it comes to the collection of personal information from children under the age of 13 years old, the Children's Online Privacy Protection Act (COPPA) puts parents in control.The Federal Trade Commission, United States' consumer protection agency,enforces the COPPA Rule, which spells out what operators of websites and online services must do toprotect children's privacy and safety online.We do not specifically market to children under the age of 13 years old.

decoder_heading: Fair Information Practices
ident_heading: Fair Information Practices
heading_content_part: Fair Information PracticesThe Fair Information Practices Principles form the backbone of privacy law in the United Statesand the concepts they include have played a significant role in the development of data protectionlaws around the globe. Understanding the Fair Information Practice Principles and how theyshould be implemented is critical to comply with the various privacy laws that protect personal information.

heading_content_part: •Within 7 business daysWe also agree to the Individual Redress Principle which requires that individuals have the right to legallypursue enforceable rights against data collectors and processors who fail to adhere to the law.This principle requires not only that individuals have enforceable rights against data users,but also that individuals have recourse to courts or government agencies to investigateand/or prosecute non-compliance by data processors.
